task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Push-Pull-Legs Split' workout routine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The wger web interface displays the workout routine named 'Push-Pull-Legs Split'.

task_completion.description: Create a new training day named 'Push 2'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A training day titled 'Push 2' is visible within the 'Push-Pull-Legs Split' routine.

task_completion.description: Add exactly 3 exercises to the 'Push 2' training day.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Push 2' training day lists exactly 3 exercises under it.

task_completion.description: Replicate the exact exercises, order, sets, and repetitions from 'Push 1' into 'Push 2'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exercises in 'Push 2' visually match the exercises in 'Push 1' in name, order, and the number of sets and repetitions.

task_completion.description: Ensure the original 'Push 1' training day is not modified or deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Push 1' is still visible in the routine alongside 'Push 2', containing its original 3 exercises.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the wger web interface in a browser, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual wger application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'localhost' and the standard wger fitness manager UI is present, not a text editor or static mockup.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open or being used to manipulate the page DOM to display 'Push 2'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target routine is 'Push-Pull-Legs Split'. The source day is 'Push 1', which contains exactly 3 exercises. The new day must be named 'Push 2' and contain the exact same 3 exercises in the same order with identical sets and reps. 'Push 1' must remain intact.
